Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{jalink|Health and Sports Day}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{jalink|February 3|Q2315}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nakanomachi, {{jalink|Mihara City|Q820760}}, {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Toyoukehime|Q2983724|Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} In the *{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}* {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{jalink|Engi (era)|Q1327274|Engi}} ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}* {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{jalink|Engi (era)|Q1327274|Engi}} ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{jalink|Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{jalink|Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in the 6th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{jalink|January 14|Q2257}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{jalink|February 3|Q2315}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{jalink|Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{jalink|February 17|Q2341}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{jalink|June 30|Q2657}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{jalink|Health and Sports Day}}) * Niiname-sai ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{jalink|Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{jalink|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karakawa-jinja}} * [https://karakawajinja.mystrikingly.com/ Karakawa Shrine] – official website ** {{Twitter|jkarakawakaraka}} ** {{Facebook|100071928734958}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chihayahiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:三原市の建築物]] [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧郷社]]

Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill||lt=Health and Sports Day|UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill||lt=Health and Sports Day|UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karakawa-jinja}} * [https://karakawajinja.mystrikingly.com/ Karakawa Shrine] – official website ** {{Twitter|jkarakawakaraka}} ** {{Facebook|100071928734958}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chihayahiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:三原市の建築物]] [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧郷社]]

Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karakawa-jinja}} * [https://karakawajinja.mystrikingly.com/ Karakawa Shrine] – official website ** {{Twitter|jkarakawakaraka}} ** {{Facebook|100071928734958}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chihayahiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:三原市の建築物]] [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧郷社]]

{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Wooden statue |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karakawa-jinja}} * [https://karakawajinja.mystrikingly.com/ Karakawa Shrine] – official website ** {{Twitter|jkarakawakaraka}} ** {{Facebook|100071928734958}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chihayahiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:三原市の建築物]] [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧郷社]]

{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Wooden statue |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karakawa-jinja}} * [https://karakawajinja.mystrikingly.com/ Karakawa Shrine] – official website ** {{Twitter|jkarakawakaraka}} ** {{Facebook|100071928734958}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chihayahiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:三原市の建築物]] [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧郷社]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karakawa-jinja}} [[JA:賀羅加波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q83886404}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Karakawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) and ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) |Location = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}{{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}} 5-10-20 Nakanomachi |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Wooden statue |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karakawa-jinja}} * [https://karakawajinja.mystrikingly.com/ Karakawa Shrine] – official website ** {{Twitter|jkarakawakaraka}} ** {{Facebook|100071928734958}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chihayahiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:三原市の建築物]] [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧郷社]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karakawa-jinja}} [[JA:賀羅加波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q83886404}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Karakawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) and ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) |Location = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}{{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}} 5-10-20 Nakanomachi |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Wooden statue |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karakawa-jinja}} * [https://karakawajinja.mystrikingly.com/ Karakawa Shrine] – official website ** {{Twitter|jkarakawakaraka}} ** {{Facebook|100071928734958}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chihayahiko Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karakawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:三原市の建築物]] [[Category:三原市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧郷社]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karakawa-jinja}} [[JA:賀羅加波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q83886404}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Karakawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) and ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) |Location = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760|1=Mihara, Hiroshima}} 5-10-20 Nakanomachi |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} |Sacred object = Wooden statue |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760|1=Mihara, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508|1=Kobayakawa Takakage}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995|1=Fukushima Masanori}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto|1=Somin Shōrai}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257|1=January 14}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959|1=Geihan Tsuushi}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karakawa-jinja}} [[JA:賀羅加波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q83886404}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Karakawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidai.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) and ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) |Location = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760|1=Mihara, Hiroshima}} 5-10-20 Nakanomachi |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}

{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} |Sacred object = Wooden statue |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Rituals = Hisha Shinji ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kara
kawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Karakawa Shrine''' (からかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|lt=Mihara City|Q820760|WD=Q820760|1=Mihara, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following sixShrine history pamphlet.: * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), only one deity is listed{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. It is said that the shrine name “Karakawa” (賀羅加波) derives from the Wakura River flowing underground near the shrine, where it was called a “dry river” (karakawa){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. There is also a theory that, based on the sound “kara,” it was a shrine of immigrant groups from the Korean Peninsula{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. The {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} is a wooden statue and is said to have been dedicated by imperial command. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as “Karakawa Shrine” in Mitsugi District of Bingo Province and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (the only Shikinaisha in Mitsugi District){{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}. According to the *Kokugunshi Shitagechō Shutsuchō*, until the time of {{ill|lt=Kobayakawa Takakage|Q1143508|WD=Q1143508|1=Kobayakawa Takakage}}, the shrine possessed 5–6 chō of shrine land nearby, but this was confiscated during the time of {{ill|lt=Fukushima Masanori|Q1194995|WD=Q1194995|1=Fukushima Masanori}}{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. It is also said that Fukushima Masanori ordered Oni Genbei to cut down a large camphor tree in the shrine precincts, and that all seven members of Oni Genbei’s family died within that year after the tree was felled{{Sfn|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Shrine grounds === File:Karakawa-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Karakawa-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Worship hall File:Karakawa-jinja, icho.jpg|Ginkgo tree (designated Natural Monument of Mihara City) === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Karakawa-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the precincts}}]] * Yadori Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Somin Shōrai|Q7560421|WD=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai-no-Mikoto|1=Somin Shōrai}}. * Takura Shrine – Enshrined deities: Yaoyorozu no Ōkami. A collective enshrinement of more than 70 shrines, including Karakawa, Sadakane, Mitsutani, Tsunenaga, Ushiroyama, Kuranouchi, Shigeta, Jūichimen, Shigemasa, Ōtani, and others. === Festivals === The annual festivals held at Karakawa Shrine are as follows: * Daily offerings festival (every day) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of each month) * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) – prayers for the New Year * Sagichō Festival / Old talisman burning festival (around {{ill|lt=January 14|Q2257|WD=Q2257|1=January 14}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}) – exorcism of misfortune, Chinowa ritual, Hisha Shinji *: Regarding the special ritual Hisha Shinji, the *{{ill|lt=Geihan Tsūshi|Q11615959|WD=Q11615959|1=Geihan Tsuushi}}* records that after shooting arrows made from plum wood toward the northeast (the demon gate), a target marked with the character for “demon” is shot, after which worshippers each shoot one arrow. * Kinen-sai ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring Grand Festival (first Sunday of April) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) – Chinowa ritual * Annual Grand Festival (October, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Niiname-sai ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great Purification, New Year’s Eve Festival, Chinka Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Mihara City === * Natural Monument ** Ginkgo tree of Karakawa Shrine – trunk circumference at breast height: 4.16 meters; tree height: 33.6 meters. Designated on February 20, 2013 (Heisei 25)“Ginkgo Tree of Karakawa Shrine,” *Cultural Properties of Mihara City*, Mihara City Board of Education, 2016.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history pamphlet * On-site explanatory sign * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office (ed.)|author=|year=1912|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Gōsha Meiji Shrine Records|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/94}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Gōsha, Lower Volume*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 94. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kunio Kuwabara|year=1980|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 22|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Karakawa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 35: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Karakawa Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === External links
